description: Workspace protocol simplification: reject workspace = "^" and "=" in manifest validation, accept only workspace = "*"
steps: Read crates/libaipm/src/manifest/validate.rs and crates/libaipm/src/manifest/error.rs
Add InvalidWorkspaceProtocol { dependency: String, protocol: String } variant to manifest error enum
Implement Display for the new error variant: "invalid workspace protocol '{protocol}' for dependency '{dependency}' — only workspace = \"*\" is supported"
Modify validate_dependencies() in validate.rs: when d.workspace.is_some(), check if the value is "*" and push InvalidWorkspaceProtocol error if not, then continue
Modify is_valid_version_req() to remove acceptance of "^" and "=" as standalone bare symbols — only "*" should be accepted
Add unit test: workspace_star_accepted — parse manifest with workspace = "*", assert valid
Add unit test: workspace_caret_rejected — parse manifest with workspace = "^", assert InvalidWorkspaceProtocol error
Add unit test: workspace_equals_rejected — parse manifest with workspace = "=", assert InvalidWorkspaceProtocol error
Update the workspace init template comment in crates/libaipm/src/workspace_init/mod.rs line 175 to reference workspace = "*" instead of workspace = "^"
Run cargo test --workspace && cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings && cargo fmt --check

description: Add glob crate dependency for workspace member pattern expansion
steps: Read Cargo.toml (workspace root) to find [workspace.dependencies] section
Add glob = "0.3" to [workspace.dependencies] in the root Cargo.toml
Add glob = { workspace = true } to [dependencies] in crates/libaipm/Cargo.toml
Run cargo build --workspace to verify the dependency resolves correctly

description: Create workspace module with error types (crates/libaipm/src/workspace/error.rs)
steps: Create directory crates/libaipm/src/workspace/
Create crates/libaipm/src/workspace/error.rs with a workspace-specific Error enum
Add variants: Discovery(String) for glob/IO/parse errors during member discovery
Add variants: NoWorkspaceRoot for when no parent with [workspace] is found
Implement std::fmt::Display and std::error::Error for the enum
Create crates/libaipm/src/workspace/mod.rs with pub mod error;
Add pub mod workspace; to crates/libaipm/src/lib.rs
Run cargo build --workspace to verify the module compiles

description: Implement find_workspace_root() — walk up filesystem to find manifest with [workspace] section
steps: Read crates/libaipm/src/workspace/mod.rs
Add use std::path::{Path, PathBuf}; and use crate::manifest;
Implement pub fn find_workspace_root(start_dir: &Path) -> Option<PathBuf> per spec section 5.2
The function loops: check current/aipm.toml exists, read it, parse with toml::from_str, check manifest.workspace.is_some(), return Some(current) if found, pop() and continue, return None if pop fails
Add unit test: find_root_from_member — create tempdir with workspace root aipm.toml (has [workspace]) and nested member dir, assert find_workspace_root from member returns root
Add unit test: find_root_returns_none — create tempdir with no workspace manifest, assert returns None
Add unit test: find_root_stops_at_first_workspace — create nested workspace roots, assert innermost is returned
Run cargo test --workspace && cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings

description: Implement WorkspaceMember struct and discover_members() — expand glob patterns to build name-to-path map
steps: Read crates/libaipm/src/workspace/mod.rs
Define pub struct WorkspaceMember with fields: name (String), path (PathBuf), version (String), manifest (manifest::types::Manifest)
Derive Debug, Clone on WorkspaceMember
Implement pub fn discover_members(workspace_root: &Path, member_patterns: &[String]) -> Result<BTreeMap<String, WorkspaceMember>, Error> per spec section 5.3
For each pattern: join with workspace_root, call glob::glob(), iterate entries
Skip non-directories, skip dirs without aipm.toml
Read and parse each member's aipm.toml via manifest::parse_and_validate()
Extract [package].name and version, error if no [package] section
Detect duplicate member names and return error
Add unit test: discover_single_glob — create workspace with members matching .ai/*, verify all discovered
Add unit test: discover_multiple_patterns — two glob patterns, verify union
Add unit test: discover_skips_non_dirs — file matches glob but is not a directory, verify skipped
Add unit test: discover_skips_no_manifest — directory matches but has no aipm.toml, verify skipped
Add unit test: discover_error_duplicate_name — two members declare same package name, verify error
Add unit test: discover_error_no_package — member manifest has no [package], verify error
Run cargo test --workspace && cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings

description: Implement split_dependencies() — partition manifest deps into workspace vs registry sets
steps: Read crates/libaipm/src/installer/pipeline.rs
Add use crate::workspace; to pipeline.rs imports if not present
Implement fn split_dependencies(manifest) -> (Vec<(String, DependencySpec)>, Vec<resolver::Dependency>) per spec section 5.4
Workspace deps are DependencySpec::Detailed(d) where d.workspace.is_some()
Registry deps use existing conversion logic (version or "*" fallback, features, default_features)
Note: DependencySpec needs Clone derived — check if it already has Clone, add if needed
Add unit test: split_deps_workspace_and_registry — manifest with both types, verify correct partition
Add unit test: split_deps_all_registry — no workspace deps, verify empty workspace vec
Add unit test: split_deps_all_workspace — only workspace deps, verify empty registry vec
Add unit test: split_deps_empty — no dependencies section, verify both empty
Run cargo test --workspace && cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings

description: Implement resolve_workspace_deps() — resolve workspace deps to local member versions with transitive support
steps: Read crates/libaipm/src/installer/pipeline.rs
Implement fn resolve_workspace_deps(workspace_dep_names, members, link_overrides) -> Result<Vec<resolver::Resolved>, Error> per spec section 5.5
Use a visited BTreeSet and queue for BFS traversal
Skip deps in link_overrides set (aipm link wins)
Look up each dep name in the members map, error if not found
Collect transitive workspace deps from member's manifest.dependencies (those with d.workspace.is_some())
Collect non-workspace transitive deps as dependency strings ("name req" format)
Parse member version via Version::parse(), produce Resolved with Source::Workspace, empty checksum, empty features
Add unit test: resolve_direct_workspace_dep — single dep, verify Resolved with Source::Workspace and correct version
Add unit test: resolve_transitive_workspace_deps — A depends on B (workspace), B depends on C (workspace), verify all three resolved
Add unit test: resolve_skips_link_overrides — dep in link_overrides set is skipped
Add unit test: resolve_error_unknown_member — dep name not in members map, verify error
Add unit test: resolve_circular_deps — A depends on B, B depends on A, verify no infinite loop (visited set handles it)
Add unit test: resolve_collects_transitive_dep_strings — verify dependencies field has "name req" strings
Run cargo test --workspace && cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings

description: Modify lockfile reconciliation to always re-resolve workspace packages
steps: Read crates/libaipm/src/lockfile/reconcile.rs
In the reconcile() function, modify the carried_forward filtering logic
Add condition: && pkg.source != "workspace" to exclude workspace packages from carried_forward
This ensures workspace deps always appear in needs_resolution, triggering re-resolution
Add unit test: workspace_packages_excluded_from_carried_forward — lockfile with workspace source, verify not in carried_forward
Add unit test: workspace_packages_in_needs_resolution — verify workspace dep appears in needs_resolution even if name matches manifest
Add unit test: registry_packages_still_carried_forward — verify non-workspace packages are unaffected
Run cargo test --workspace && cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings

description: Add workspace_root field to InstallConfig and wire up workspace context in install()
steps: Read crates/libaipm/src/installer/pipeline.rs — the InstallConfig struct and install() function
Add pub workspace_root: Option<PathBuf> field to InstallConfig
Update all existing InstallConfig construction sites (in main.rs cmd_install and in tests) to include workspace_root: None initially
In install(), after manifest loading and lockfile loading, add workspace discovery step
If workspace_root is Some or if manifest has workspace deps, call find_workspace_root() and discover_members()
Call split_dependencies() instead of manifest_to_resolver_deps()
Load link overrides from link_state_path via link_state::list()
Call resolve_workspace_deps() for workspace deps
Pass only registry deps to the existing resolver
Merge workspace + registry resolutions into a single Resolution
Run cargo build --workspace to verify compilation

description: Modify install loop to handle Source::Workspace with directory linking instead of tarball download
steps: Read crates/libaipm/src/installer/pipeline.rs — the fetch-store-link loop (lines 139-180)
Replace the unconditional registry.download() call with a match on resolved.source
For Source::Workspace: call directory_link::create(member_path, plugins_dir/name) and gitignore::add_entry()
For Source::Registry: keep existing tarball fetch, store, link_package(), gitignore logic
For Source::Path: skip for now (not implemented, log a warning)
Ensure the members BTreeMap is available in scope (passed from the workspace discovery step)
Ensure create_dir_all is called on plugins_dir before workspace linking (it may not exist yet)
Add unit test: workspace_dep_creates_directory_link — verify symlink/junction is created from member path to plugins dir
Add unit test: workspace_dep_updates_gitignore — verify package name appears in managed gitignore section
Add unit test: workspace_dep_in_lockfile — verify lockfile entry has source = "workspace" and empty checksum
Run cargo test --workspace && cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings

description: Wire up workspace root discovery in CLI cmd_install() and pass workspace_root to InstallConfig
steps: Read crates/aipm/src/main.rs — cmd_install() function
After resolving the working directory, check if the manifest at that dir has a [workspace] section
If not, call workspace::find_workspace_root() to walk up and find the workspace root
Set workspace_root in InstallConfig to the discovered root path (or None if not in a workspace)
If workspace root is found and differs from current dir, log an info message about the discovered root
Run cargo build --workspace to verify compilation
Manual test: create a workspace with two members, run aipm install from the root, verify both members are linked

description: Handle workspace deps in --locked mode: use lockfile resolution but still create directory links
steps: Read the --locked mode path in installer/pipeline.rs
In --locked mode, build_resolution_from_lockfile() already produces Source::Workspace for lockfile entries with source = "workspace"
Verify that the source-aware install loop (feature 10) correctly handles Source::Workspace entries from lockfile reconstruction
In --locked mode, workspace discovery must still run to get member paths for linking (the lockfile has versions but not paths)
Add unit test: locked_mode_workspace_deps_linked — verify workspace deps from lockfile get directory links
Add unit test: locked_mode_validates_workspace_deps — verify lockfile validation includes workspace dep names
Run cargo test --workspace && cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings

description: Update BDD feature scenarios for workspace protocol changes
steps: Read tests/features/monorepo/orchestration.feature
Update scenario 'Reference a workspace sibling with workspace protocol' (line 106-115) to use workspace = "*" instead of workspace = "^"
Convert 'Workspace protocol is replaced on publish' scenario (line 117-121) — since publish is out of scope, convert to a workspace = "*" linking scenario or mark as @wip
Convert 'Workspace protocol with exact version' scenario (line 123-127) — convert to a validation error test for workspace = "="
Convert 'Workspace protocol with wildcard' scenario (line 129-133) — already uses "*", keep as the valid case
Add new scenario: workspace = "^" rejected with validation error
Add new scenario: workspace = "=" rejected with validation error
Verify BDD test runner includes the updated feature file if applicable (check bdd.rs filter)
Run cargo test --workspace

description: Implement tracing/logging for workspace dependency resolution observability
steps: Read crates/libaipm/src/workspace/mod.rs
Add tracing::info! when discover_members() finds members — log count and member names
Add tracing::info! in resolve_workspace_deps() when resolving each dep — log name, version, path
Add tracing::debug! when skipping a dep due to aipm link override
Add tracing::warn! in discover_members() when a directory matches a glob but has no aipm.toml
Run cargo build --workspace && cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings

description: Run full build, test, clippy, fmt, and coverage gate to verify all changes pass quality checks
steps: Run cargo fmt --check to verify formatting
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings to verify no lint warnings
Run cargo build --workspace to verify clean compilation
Run cargo test --workspace to verify all tests pass
Run cargo +nightly llvm-cov clean --workspace
Run cargo +nightly llvm-cov --no-report --workspace --branch
Run cargo +nightly llvm-cov --no-report --doc
Run cargo +nightly llvm-cov report --doctests --branch --ignore-filename-regex '(tests/|research/|specs/|wizard_tty\.rs)' and verify >= 89% branch coverage
Fix any coverage gaps by adding additional unit tests for uncovered branches
